Yes it's true both are very unlikely to win. But both are +ev according to the models I follow.

Both can clinch with a win.

In the end it's all gambling so I guess I can afford to gamble a small amount after recent victories.

Breakeven election day - Alan Wilson profits wiped out with New York primary losses.

We live to fight another day. 🇺🇸

Re: California Billionaire Tax

This has been a strange market and a weird trade. I’ll most likely hold my shares until maturity—zero-or-hero style.

Basically, SEIU, the union sponsoring the bill, has until June 25 at 5 PM Pacific to withdraw it. They have already successfully completed the signature verification process, meaning the measure has qualified for the ballot.

The thing is, SEIU is a very corrupt union that has previously accepted favors and bribes for its own gain. Newsom has been working hard to negotiate a deal that would convince them to withdraw the measure, as reported by Politico and Bloomberg.

Twice, the NO side (the bill not appearing on the November ballot) was pumped to 90¢ on Kalshi. It always seemed like someone knew something was about to happen, but that same pricing never carried over to Polymarket, so I couldn’t sell my position.

Anyway, either I win or holding to zero.

Two days to find out.
